sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — NES / Contra
sources.notes: Primary reference. Gromaides boss arm physics documented here (0x0.B5 pixels/frame). Player physics described qualitatively.

sources.name: Contra Speedrunning Wiki (Fandom)
sources.notes: Movement mechanics, weapon interactions, stage-specific physics.

sources.name: Data Crystal — Contra
sources.notes: RAM map. Physics constants less thoroughly documented than SMB or Mega Man.

notes: Contra has significantly less precise ROM-level documentation than the Mario or Mega Man games.
Most documented values are from speedrunning community frame-counting rather than disassembly.
Contra uses different physics in its three stage types: side-scrolling, top-down (base), and vertically-scrolling.
Side-scrolling stages: standard platformer physics.
Top-down stages (Stages 2, 4 — 'base stages'): 8-directional movement, no jump, screen-by-screen transitions.
Vertically-scrolling stage (Stage 5, waterfall): same as side-scrolling but with forced upward scroll.
NO acceleration: players move at constant speed (like Castlevania, unlike Mario).
Jump is NOT variable height — committed on press.
Contra has no walk/run distinction — players always move at one constant speed.
Speed is approximately 1.5 px/frame based on community measurement.
VERIFY against Data Crystal RAM map or disassembly.

side_scrolling_stages.notes: No acceleration — constant velocity from the moment direction is pressed. Stopping is instant.

initial_y_velocity_approx.notes: Approximate from community measurement. VERIFY against ROM.

jump_side_scrolling.source: Community documentation: 'Contra's jump is non-variable — holding jump does NOT extend height. The arc is committed on button press.'
notes: Jump height is FIXED — cannot be extended by holding A.
Horizontal direction CAN be changed mid-air freely — full air control.
This combination (fixed height, free direction) is unusual. Most games with fixed jump also have locked direction (Castlevania) or full variable height.
8-directional aiming is available while jumping.

gravity.notes: Approximate. Contra's jump arc is relatively fast compared to Mario. VERIFY.

aiming_system.description: 8-directional aiming: up-left, up, up-right, right, down-right (jump only), down (jump only), down-left (jump only), left
aiming_system.input_method: D-pad held in aiming direction simultaneously with move/fire inputs
notes: The 8-directional aiming is Contra's signature mechanic. Players can aim diagonally while standing and shoot in all 8 directions while jumping.
Down/lower directions are only accessible while jumping — cannot aim downward while standing.
Prone (hold Down on ground) allows low-angle aiming and reduces hitbox.

prone.notes: Prone is essential for avoiding certain enemy attacks and repositioning under fire. Cannot move — it's a stationary combat stance.

fireball.label: F
fireball.notes: Slow bouncing projectile. Useful for clearing floor-level enemies.

laser.label: L
laser.notes: Laser fires instantaneously across the screen. Extremely powerful — kills most enemies in one hit. Limited to 1 on screen at a time, creating a fire-rate tradeoff.

spread_gun.label: S
spread_gun.notes: 5 bullets fire simultaneously in a fan pattern. Center bullet goes straight; pairs at ~±30° and ~±60°. The most powerful weapon in most situations due to coverage. VERIFY exact angles.

rapid_bullet.label: RB
rapid_bullet.notes: Rapid fire variant. Exact fire rate vs machine gun needs verification.

pickup_system.mechanism: Shoot the Falcon Pod (red helicopter) to reveal a capsule icon; touch the icon to pick up
pickup_system.notes: One weapon at a time. The helicopter circles continuously. Shooting the pod reveals the power-up icon on the ground.

climbing.input: hold Up or Down on rope to climb
climbing.dismount: press jump to release and launch off rope
climbing.gravity_while_holding: suspended — no falling while holding rope

notes: ONE HIT KILL — any contact with enemy, projectile, or hazard kills immediately.
This makes Contra a reaction and positioning game, not a health management game.
The Konami Code (Up Up Down Down Left Right Left Right B A Start) gives 30 lives.

standing.notes: Narrower than the sprite. Exact values need ROM verification. Contra is known for relatively forgiving hitboxes relative to sprite size.

hitboxes.notes: Contra hitboxes are intentionally narrower than sprites — the game is punishing enough with one-hit-kill mechanics.

top_down_stages.objective: destroy base components to progress to next screen
notes: Top-down stages are structurally different from side-scrolling: no jump, 8-directional movement, stationary camera until reaching screen edge.
These stages break up the run-and-gun pacing with a different tactical challenge.
Enemy spawns and object placement create flanking threats from multiple directions.

waterfall_stage.death_condition: falling off bottom of screen = instant death
waterfall_stage.notes: Stage 5 is a vertically-scrolling variation. Same physics as horizontal stages but the camera forces upward movement. Falling behind the scroll kills immediately.

arm_movement_speed.source: TASVideos: 'Adds ±0x0.B5 pixels to x-coordinate and −0x0.B5 pixels to y-coordinate'
arm_movement_speed.notes: 0xB5 = 181. 181/256 ≈ 0.707 px/frame. This is approximately √2/2, which gives exactly 45-degree extension. This is a ROM-confirmed value from TASVideos.

boss_hp_limit.description: Boss HP can only drop 1 HP per frame maximum
boss_hp_limit.source: TASVideos: 'Boss HP can only drop 1 HP per frame'
boss_hp_limit.notes: This limits how fast bosses can be killed regardless of weapon. Important for speedrun routing.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The one-hit-kill system fundamentally changes level design philosophy. Every enemy placement is life-threatening. Enemy density, projectile speed, and timing must be calibrated around instant death.
8-directional aiming makes Contra a different genre from Mario — it's a 'shooter within a platformer.' Level design must create scenarios where aiming direction matters as much as positioning.

Fixed jump height with full air control is a unique combination. The player can reposition horizontally mid-jump but cannot extend the arc. This makes jump timing about starting height (when to jump) rather than hold duration.
The weapon system creates a strong economy around the helicopter pods. Players make risk/reward decisions about when to expose themselves to grab a weapon upgrade.
The two-player simultaneous design means Contra levels work with shared screen space. Levels are wider than average to accommodate two players without constant overlap.
